title: libpod: don't warn about cgroupsv1 on FreeBSD
body:
This factors out the check for cgroupsv2 unified mode into a platform-specific file and stops podman from generating a (harmless) warning every time it is run on FreeBSD.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: If possible, I would like this change in the v5.0 branch as well so that later releases from that branch have the fix.
@mheon in general, im ok with this ... but did a build flag ever get set for this?  seems like we could just build tag it out on freebsd ?
We ended up never doing a build-tag, we were going to patch the code out on RHEL9. In retrospect, a build tag for the warning would have been a good idea, maybe?

I would be fine with a build tag if that's your preferred solution. On the other hand, cgroups is very unlikely to be supported on any non-linux platform so it seemed to make sense to make this check platform-specific.

One possible alternative would be to have IsCgroup2UnifiedMode return true on FreeBSD but that seems confusing and wrong to me.
I think this implementation is fine. We can discuss further if we want a buildtag on Linux specifically to prevent CGv1-only distros from printing warnings, but we did discuss that before.
The linter errors are wierd, the Runtime type is definitely defined inside of Libpod.

@mheon: once the present PR merges, I will cherry-pick it on top of v5.0 in a new PR and assign it to you.
